insights.headline: PROCURE: Pet Care — source from Indonesia (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Increase Pet Care procurement from Indonesia, allocating a growing share of volume away from Thailand and China.** Indonesia is the only top origin showing positive supply growth (+19.0%) and is classified as emerging, while the dominant origins — Thailand (51.3% share, -35.2% growth) and China (26.2% share, -30.9% growth) — are both contracting, signalling tightening availability and potential supply risk. Demand signals on Instagram and AliExpress show strong positive momentum (+34.99 and +34.74 respectively), justifying active procurement action, and shifting toward Indonesia also provides a differentiated sourcing position versus key competitor Rewe, which is anchored to Thailand.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Beauty & Cosmetics — source from South Korea (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Prioritise procurement of Beauty & Cosmetics from South Korea, with secondary sourcing from China and Japan.** South Korea holds the largest supply share at 45.4% and is classified as an emerging, high-growth origin, making it the strongest anchor for range development; China (34.6% share) and Japan (11.7% share) provide meaningful volume diversification at comparable growth trajectories. Demand is accelerating sharply on Pinterest (momentum 4.95, acceleration 1.51) and gaining on Instagram, signalling rising aspirational and visual-discovery-driven consumer interest in Germany that K-beauty and J-beauty aesthetics are well-positioned to capture — and with Edeka already active in South Korean sourcing, moving decisively now is essential to maintain competitive relevance.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Knitwear & Apparel — source from China
insights.narrative: **Procure Knitwear & Apparel now, prioritising China as primary origin (31.9% import share), with Vietnam and Bangladesh as secondary sources, to capture accelerating social-driven demand before competitors consolidate position.** Pinterest momentum is strongly positive (46.5) with the highest acceleration in the dataset (10.8), and Instagram signals are also rising, indicating genuine early-stage consumer pull in the German market. China's dominant supply share makes it the most reliable volume lever despite contracting import growth, while sourcing from Vietnam and Bangladesh provides diversification and differentiation from key rivals—Aldi, Tesco, Auchan, and Sainsbury's are all concentrated in Bangladesh, leaving Lidl with a structural advantage by leading on China-origin product assortment.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Small Kitchen Appliances — source from China
insights.narrative: **Procure Small Kitchen Appliances primarily from China, with secondary volumes from Thailand and South Korea.** China commands an 83.3% import share and remains the dominant, most scalable origin for this category, and all key competitors — Aldi, Edeka, and Walmart — are already sourcing from there, making it essential for Lidl to maintain competitive positioning. Demand signals support the procurement decision: AliExpress shows strong momentum (25.2) with positive growth (2.6), and Pinterest is accelerating (acceleration: 2.5), together representing 2 of 3 rising demand sources, while Thailand and South Korea offer meaningful secondary diversification despite their smaller shares (6.5% and 2.8% respectively). Note that supply-side import growth is declining across all origins, so volumes should be calibrated conservatively against actual sell-through forecasts rather than scaled aggressively.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Footwear — source from Vietnam
insights.narrative: **Prioritise footwear procurement from Vietnam (44.1% supply share), with a secondary allocation from China (42.0%) and a smaller volume from Indonesia (9.2%) to diversify supply risk.** Demand signals are mixed but Instagram shows meaningful positive momentum (11.6) with continued growth (2.2), indicating a live consumer appetite in Germany worth stocking into, while Vietnam's supply dominance and superior share versus all other origins makes it the most defensible primary source despite category-wide supply contraction. Competitive intelligence reinforces this call — Aldi, Carrefour, Mercadona, and Walmart are all sourcing footwear from Vietnam, confirming it as the market-standard origin, while China and Indonesia round out the recommended mix as established secondaries already utilised by Tesco and Auchan respectively.

insights.headline: WATCH: Other
insights.narrative:
## Procurement Recommendation: WATCH – Other Category

**Hold procurement and do not commit to new volume at this time.** The demand signal is highly mixed and insufficiently validated: only 1 of 3 active sources is rising (Instagram, momentum +43.9, growth +10%), while TikTok shows meaningful negative momentum (−8.49) and acceleration (−0.94), and Pinterest is effectively flat with negligible movement. AliExpress marketplace data is entirely zero, providing no transactional confirmation of consumer intent translating to purchase. No origin markets, recommended suppliers, or competitor benchmarks are present in the evidence, making it impossible to responsibly direct sourcing to any specific geography or supply chain.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Drinkware & Tumblers — source from China
insights.narrative: **Procure Drinkware & Tumblers primarily from China, with a supplementary watch on Thailand.** China dominates supply with a 99.07% import share and remains the structurally indispensable source despite a slight volume contraction (-5.29% growth), making it the only viable primary origin at scale; Tesco is already sourcing from the same origin, confirming its competitive relevance. Demand is driven by strong Instagram momentum (37.34, +4.87% growth) — the sole rising signal across five platforms — justifying a targeted, social-led procurement volume rather than a broad range expansion, while Thailand's emerging share growth (+38.15%) warrants monitoring as a potential secondary or risk-mitigation source in future cycles.

insights.headline: WATCH: Drinkware & Kitchen
insights.narrative:
## Procurement Recommendation: Drinkware & Kitchen

**Proceed with cautious, targeted procurement for visually driven drinkware items, prioritising Instagram-trending formats, while deferring volume commitments until supply origins can be confirmed.**

Demand signals are mixed but notable: Instagram shows strong positive momentum (4.7) with high acceleration (10.88), suggesting an emerging consumer interest trend worth acting on, while TikTok signals are currently negative and AliExpress marketplace activity is flat — indicating the opportunity is early-stage and platform-specific rather than broadly established. However, with no recommended or confirmed supply origins available in the current data, procurement should be limited to existing verified suppliers already within Lidl's supply base, avoiding any new origin commitments until sourcing intelligence is available to ground selection decisions.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Toys & Collectibles — source from China
insights.narrative: **Prioritise procurement of Toys & Collectibles from China as the primary origin, complemented by selective sourcing from Vietnam and Indonesia as secondary suppliers.** China dominates supply with an 86.25% import share and is the established origin for all identified competitors (Tesco, Ahold Delhaize, Walmart), making it the essential anchor for competitive parity and supply depth, despite a category-wide supply growth contraction of -26.07%. Partially offset concentration risk by allocating residual volume to Vietnam (8.47% share, -21.93% growth) and Indonesia (2.73% share, -20.29% growth), both of which show comparatively shallower supply declines and are already recommended origins — noting that demand signals are broadly weak across platforms except for a meaningful Instagram momentum spike (+5.41, +84.08% growth), which warrants a lean initial buy rather than deep stock commitment until broader demand recovery is confirmed.

insights.headline: WATCH: Coffee — source from Vietnam
insights.narrative: **Prioritise sourcing coffee from Vietnam, with secondary volumes from Indonesia and India.** Vietnam commands the dominant supply share (62%) and is the only top origin showing positive supply growth (+7.2%), making it the most reliable and scalable procurement base; Indonesia (24% share) and India (12% share) provide meaningful volume as diversification buffers despite slight share declines. Demand signals are mixed — AliExpress shows positive momentum and growth, while TikTok and Pinterest are declining — but Instagram's sharp acceleration (+3.80) warrants monitoring as a leading indicator of emerging consumer interest that may support incremental volume commitments from Vietnam, where competitor Rewe is already active.

insights.headline: WATCH: Audio & Earbuds — source from China
insights.narrative: **Procure Audio & Earbuds primarily from China, with a secondary allocation from Vietnam, as these two origins hold the dominant supply share (61.8% and 33.0% respectively) and offer the most established sourcing base among all available options.** Demand signals are broadly weak — TikTok and AliExpress show steep negative momentum, and Pinterest is declining sharply — with only Instagram showing modest positive momentum (0.44), indicating a category in contraction rather than growth; procurement volumes should therefore be conservative and closely tied to confirmed sell-through forecasts. China remains the strategic anchor given its commanding share, alignment with competitor sourcing (both Aldi and Ahold Delhaize source from China), and relatively lower supply-side decline compared to Vietnam and Thailand, making it the most defensible origin for maintaining cost efficiency and supply reliability in a softening demand environment.

insights.headline: HOLD: Tea & Matcha — source from Japan (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Maintain current Tea & Matcha procurement volumes but pivot sourcing toward Japan and Pakistan while avoiding expansion into India, China, or Indonesia.** Demand signals are uniformly negative across all five tracked platforms — with particularly sharp declines on TikTok and Pinterest — and no rising sources are recorded, justifying a HOLD rather than a volume increase. However, Japan holds the largest origin share (34.7%) with the strongest supply-side growth (+26.4%) and emerging status, while Pakistan offers a secondary emerging option with comparable growth (+24.0%) at a small but rising share, providing a differentiated sourcing mix versus competitors Rewe (China) and Sainsbury's (India), whose origins are both in supply decline.

insights.headline: HOLD: Packaging & Shipping Supplies
insights.narrative: **Maintain current procurement volumes for Packaging & Shipping Supplies without expanding commitments or sourcing new origins at this time.** Demand signals are weak to negative across all measured platforms, with TikTok showing particularly sharp negative momentum (−9.16) and negative acceleration (−1.15), while Pinterest, though decelerating less severely, also trends downward; no platform is registering positive growth. No recommended origins, rising sources, or competitor activity are identified in the evidence, leaving no supply-side rationale to justify incremental purchasing beyond existing contracted volumes.

insights.headline: HOLD: Candles & Home Fragrance — source from Vietnam
insights.narrative: **Maintain current Vietnam-sourced volumes for Candles & Home Fragrance without expansion, while avoiding any increase in commitments from China or India.** Demand signals are broadly negative across all active platforms — TikTok and AliExpress show sharp momentum declines and TikTok is the only source registering negative acceleration — with zero rising demand sources out of two tracked, indicating no near-term volume upside to justify scaling procurement. Vietnam remains the dominant and most stable supply origin (69% share, minimal volume decline of -3.4% versus China's -22% and India's -25%), making it the appropriate anchor source to hold, while China and India should be deprioritised despite being algorithmically recommended given their steeper supply-side erosion and Carrefour's existing presence in the India lane.

insights.headline: HOLD: Sports & Outdoors
insights.narrative: **Hold current inventory levels and defer any new procurement commitments for Sports & Outdoors until demand signals stabilise.** All three active demand channels show negative momentum and growth — TikTok in particular signals severe deterioration (momentum: -9.30, acceleration: -0.95) — with zero rising sources out of three tracked, indicating broad-based and accelerating softness rather than a platform-specific anomaly. No recommended origins or top origins are identified in the evidence, making new sourcing decisions premature and unjustifiable at this time.

insights.headline: HOLD: Apparel & Fashion
insights.narrative: **Maintain current Apparel & Fashion procurement volumes without expansion or reduction until clearer demand signals emerge.** The evidence shows zero positive momentum across all tracked platforms, with TikTok registering active decline (momentum: -6.97, growth: -8.6%) and Instagram also trending negative — indicating broad consumer interest softness across the EU. No rising demand sources, no recommended origins, and no competitor activity are identified in the data, removing any basis for opportunistic volume increases or origin diversification at this time.

insights.headline: HOLD: Electronics & Accessories
insights.narrative: **Maintain current Electronics & Accessories procurement volumes without expansion or reduction at this time.** Demand signals are broadly negative across all measured platforms—TikTok shows the steepest decline (momentum: -6.63, growth: -99.6%), with Google Trends, Instagram, and Pinterest all confirming sustained contraction in consumer interest. No rising demand sources, no recommended origins, and no competitive activity are identified in the evidence, meaning there is no data-supported case to increase orders or diversify sourcing at this point.
